page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the server includes a massive 60,000 GB NVMe SSD array, delivering high-density, low-latency local storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark testing, the i7ie.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance. It achieves top 10% placement in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU scores, and Passmark database operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. Memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations register in the top 25%, showing strong throughput across intensive workloads.

This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments by consolidating massive storage and memory resources onto a single physical node, reducing the need for distributed clustering. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for active deep learning training, though it performs strongly in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. It is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, NoSQL stores, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive ETL pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz, it features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a massive 60,000 GB local NVMe SSD array. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for database operations, static web serving, and multi-core CPU performance, with strong memory bandwidth and cryptographic execution. The integration of Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets further accelerates vector and matrix calculations. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and intensive ETL processing where massive local storage and high memory capacity are critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 Intel vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six Intel vCPUs and sixty terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity.
bullet_points: 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.6 GHz with AVX-512 and AMX support
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 8 GB per core
60,000 GB of low-latency local NVMe SSD storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and static web serving
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and ETL pipelines
